question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamp query with `lowerBound == Long.MAX_VALUE - 1` and operator `GT` so that `getAdjustedLowerRangeValue()` overflows to `Long.MIN_VALUE`, causing `minTimestamp > maxTimestamp` to evaluate as false and routing the query to `accountBalanceRepository.getSupplyHistory()` with a corrupted range, returning a zero `consensusTimestamp` that triggers `EntityNotFoundException` and permanently denies supply data to downstream fund-release systems?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a timestamp `Bound` where `upper.value() == 0` and `upper.operator() == LT`, causing `adjustUpperBound()` to decrement to `-1` (underflow to `Long.MAX_VALUE - 1` if cast), so that `maxTimestamp` becomes a huge positive value and `getFirstDayOfMonth(maxTimestamp, -1)` computes an `optimalLowerBound` far in the future, forcing `Math.max(minTimestamp, optimalLowerBound)` to push `minTimestamp` beyond `maxTimestamp`, resulting in an empty supply query that returns `consensusTimestamp == 0` and throws `EntityNotFoundException`, freezing any downstream fund-release logic that depends on this endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp range where `minTimestamp` equals `maxTimestamp` exactly at a nanosecond boundary that falls between two consecutive `account_balance` snapshots, causing `getSupplyHistory()` to return `consensusTimestamp == 0`, triggering `EntityNotFoundException` and causing any wallet or exchange that polls this endpoint to treat all balances as frozen?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user provide a timestamp `Bound` with `lower.operator() == GT` and `lower.value() == Long.MAX_VALUE` combined with `upper.operator() == LT` and `upper.value() == 1`, so that after adjustment `minTimestamp` overflows to `Long.MIN_VALUE` and `maxTimestamp` becomes `0`, causing `Math.max(minTimestamp, optimalLowerBound)` to select `optimalLowerBound` which is still larger than `maxTimestamp`, resulting in an empty supply result with `consensusTimestamp == 0` that permanently breaks fund-release calculations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getFirstDayOfMonth(long timestamp, int monthOffset)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `maxTimestamp` value near `Long.MAX_VALUE` nanoseconds (year ~2262), causing `Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, timestamp)` to throw `DateTimeException` due to nanosecond overflow in `Instant` construction, resulting in an unhandled exception that crashes the supply endpoint and permanently denies fund-release data to downstream systems?
